name: Hub.Broadcast concurrent race safety
aim: 50 concurrent Broadcast callers + 1 GetLastPayload poller + 1 ClientCount poller for 20s: zero races, zero panics, SequenceNo never 0 after first broadcast

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map/struct access in Broadcast must be fully protected by h.mu

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: RingBuffer memory stability under 10M pushes
aim: Pushing 10000000 entries into RingBuffer(capacity=1000) must not grow HeapInuse beyond 2× baseline

threshold.rationale: Fixed-size ring buffer must overwrite old slots; heap growth > 2× means entries are being retained

on_exceed: RingBuffer retains pushed MetricPoints beyond its capacity → heap grows O(N) → Kubernetes OOM kill → pod CrashLoopBackOff → total telemetry loss

answered_questions.what_was_tested: RingBuffer(capacity=1000).Push called 10000000 times; HeapInuse sampled every 1000000 pushes with forced GC
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 2× allows for GC overhead and Go runtime internals; any larger growth means entries are escaping the fixed-size ring
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Memory grows proportionally to ingestion volume → OOM kill in production after hours of operation
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: N/A — single-goroutine memory test; run TestL3_TEL_001 for concurrent safety
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: runtime.MemStats.HeapInuse before and after 10M pushes with GC forced between samples
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Single-writer sequential push to verify allocation semantics, not concurrent safety

name: RingBuffer concurrent Push/Snapshot/Last/Size/SummaryStats
aim: 10 writers + 10 readers on one RingBuffer for 30s: zero data races (race detector), zero torn reads, zero panics

threshold.rationale: Any torn read means the control loop sees a physically impossible sensor state

error_messages: pushes_done=564169 snapshots=1806376 last_calls=13163200 size_calls=743477 summary_calls=743477 final_size=1024 mean_req=56608.25

on_exceed: Torn MetricPoint delivered to QueuePhysicsEngine → MPC optimises on garbled state → unbounded ControlDirective.ScaleFactor sent to actuator

answered_questions.what_was_tested: RingBuffer(capacity=1024) under 10 concurrent writers and 10 concurrent readers for 30s, 564169 total pushes
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any negative RequestRate or zero Timestamp in a returned entry means a partial write was observed — the sync.RWMutex failed to protect the slot
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: QueuePhysicsEngine.RunQueueModel receives MetricPoint with garbage state → CollapseRisk computed incorrectly → phaseRuntime.mergeDirective produces wrong ScaleFactor
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector (-race flag on test binary) — any race causes non-zero exit
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not the focus of this test
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW: multiple concurrent writers via Push (exclusive lock) + multiple concurrent readers via Snapshot/Last/Size/SummaryStats (shared lock)

answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW: multiple concurrent writers via Push (exclusive lock) + multiple concurrent readers via Snapshot/Last/Size/SummaryStats (shared lock)

name: RingBuffer concurrent Push/Snapshot/Last/Size/SummaryStats
aim: 10 writers + 10 readers on one RingBuffer for 30s: zero data races (race detector), zero torn reads, zero panics

threshold.rationale: Any torn read means the control loop sees a physically impossible sensor state

error_messages: pushes_done=954 snapshots=929715 last_calls=19263784 size_calls=332766 summary_calls=332766 final_size=954 mean_req=48.33

on_exceed: Torn MetricPoint delivered to QueuePhysicsEngine → MPC optimises on garbled state → unbounded ControlDirective.ScaleFactor sent to actuator

answered_questions.what_was_tested: RingBuffer(capacity=1024) under 10 concurrent writers and 10 concurrent readers for 30s, 954 total pushes
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any negative RequestRate or zero Timestamp in a returned entry means a partial write was observed — the sync.RWMutex failed to protect the slot
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: QueuePhysicsEngine.RunQueueModel receives MetricPoint with garbage state → CollapseRisk computed incorrectly → phaseRuntime.mergeDirective produces wrong ScaleFactor
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector (-race flag on test binary) — any race causes non-zero exit

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Hub.Broadcast concurrent race safety
aim: 50 concurrent Broadcast callers + 1 GetLastPayload poller + 1 ClientCount poller for 20s: zero races, zero panics, SequenceNo never 0 after first broadcast

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map/struct access in Broadcast must be fully protected by h.mu

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Hub.Broadcast concurrent race safety
aim: 50 concurrent Broadcast callers + 1 GetLastPayload poller + 1 ClientCount poller for 20s: zero races, zero panics, SequenceNo never 0 after first broadcast

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map/struct access in Broadcast must be fully protected by h.mu

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

name: RingBuffer memory stability under 10M pushes
aim: Pushing 10000000 entries into RingBuffer(capacity=1000) must not grow HeapInuse beyond 2× baseline

threshold.rationale: Fixed-size ring buffer must overwrite old slots; heap growth > 2× means entries are being retained

on_exceed: RingBuffer retains pushed MetricPoints beyond its capacity → heap grows O(N) → Kubernetes OOM kill → pod CrashLoopBackOff → total telemetry loss

answered_questions.what_was_tested: RingBuffer(capacity=1000).Push called 10000000 times; HeapInuse sampled every 1000000 pushes with forced GC
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 2× allows for GC overhead and Go runtime internals; any larger growth means entries are escaping the fixed-size ring
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Memory grows proportionally to ingestion volume → OOM kill in production after hours of operation
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: N/A — single-goroutine memory test; run TestL3_TEL_001 for concurrent safety
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: runtime.MemStats.HeapInuse before and after 10M pushes with GC forced between samples
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Single-writer sequential push to verify allocation semantics, not concurrent safety

name: Store concurrent Ingest/Prune/AllWindows/Window/ServiceIDs
aim: 20 ingesters + 5 pruners + 5 readers on Store for 30s: zero races, zero panics, zero nil windows

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map access without proper locking causes panic in Go runtime

on_exceed: Race on Store.buffers map → 'concurrent map read and map write' panic → Orchestrator.tick goroutine crashes → control loop halts → system goes open-loop

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Store(bufCap=256 maxSvc=50 staleAge=2s) under 20 ingesters + 5 pruners + 5 readers for 30s
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Go's map is not concurrency-safe; unguarded concurrent access panics the runtime — not a data race, an outright crash
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: The Orchestrator tick goroutine panics on map access → entire control loop stops → system runs open-loop indefinitely
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on test binary + explicit panic recovery in each goroutine
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — not the focus of this test
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Double-checked locking (RLock for read, upgrade to Lock for write) + delete-under-lock in Prune racing with Ingest

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Store(bufCap=256 maxSvc=50 staleAge=2s) under 20 ingesters + 5 pruners + 5 readers for 30s
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Go's map is not concurrency-safe; unguarded concurrent access panics the runtime — not a data race, an outright crash
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: The Orchestrator tick goroutine panics on map access → entire control loop stops → system runs open-loop indefinitely
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on test binary + explicit panic recovery in each goroutine
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — not the focus of this test
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Double-checked locking (RLock for read, upgrade to Lock for write) + delete-under-lock in Prune racing with Ingest

name: Store cardinality cap enforced under concurrent ingestion
aim: 50 goroutines each registering unique services against Store(maxSvc=10): count must never exceed cap

threshold.rationale: Exceeding maxServices allows unbounded map growth → OOM in high-cardinality environments

error_messages: goroutines=50 iterations_each=500 total_ingests=25000 violations=0 panics=0 final_count=10 max_allowed=10

on_exceed: More than maxServices entries in the Store → heap grows unboundedly → Kubernetes OOM killer terminates the pod

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 50 goroutines trying to register 50 unique services against Store(maxSvc=10) simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Cap must be a hard guarantee; exceeding it by even one service breaks the memory budget calculation
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Production system with high service cardinality fills memory → OOM kill → control loop restarts → brief open-loop operation
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on test binary detects race on len(s.buffers) check vs map write
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: TOCTOU (Time-of-Check-Time-of-Use) race: check len(s.buffers) then add — must be guarded by single Lock across both operations

aim: 50 goroutines each registering unique services against Store(maxSvc=10): count must never exceed cap

threshold.rationale: Exceeding maxServices allows unbounded map growth → OOM in high-cardinality environments

error_messages: goroutines=50 iterations_each=500 total_ingests=25000 violations=0 panics=0 final_count=10 max_allowed=10

on_exceed: More than maxServices entries in the Store → heap grows unboundedly → Kubernetes OOM killer terminates the pod

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 50 goroutines trying to register 50 unique services against Store(maxSvc=10) simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Cap must be a hard guarantee; exceeding it by even one service breaks the memory budget calculation
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Production system with high service cardinality fills memory → OOM kill → control loop restarts → brief open-loop operation
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on test binary detects race on len(s.buffers) check vs map write
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A

name: Store cardinality cap enforced under concurrent ingestion
aim: 50 goroutines each registering unique services against Store(maxSvc=10): count must never exceed cap

on_exceed: More than maxServices entries in the Store → heap grows unboundedly → Kubernetes OOM killer terminates the pod

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Cap must be a hard guarantee; exceeding it by even one service breaks the memory budget calculation

answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on test binary detects race on len(s.buffers) check vs map write
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: TOCTOU (Time-of-Check-Time-of-Use) race: check len(s.buffers) then add — must be guarded by single Lock across both operations

name: Hub.Broadcast concurrent race safety
aim: 50 concurrent Broadcast callers + 1 GetLastPayload poller + 1 ClientCount poller for 20s: zero races, zero panics, SequenceNo never 0 after first broadcast

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map/struct access in Broadcast must be fully protected by h.mu

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map/struct access in Broadcast must be fully protected by h.mu

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly

answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select

name: Slow client dropped without blocking fast client
aim: After 26 broadcasts with 1 slow + 1 fast client: slow dropped, fast receives ≥16 msgs, no broadcast blocks

threshold.rationale: Broadcast must use non-blocking send (select default) — never block on a slow consumer

on_exceed: Broadcast blocks on slow client → all Broadcast calls serialise behind slow consumer → dashboard latency grows unboundedly for ALL operators, not just the slow one

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached

name: Hub.Broadcast concurrent race safety
aim: 50 concurrent Broadcast callers + 1 GetLastPayload poller + 1 ClientCount poller for 20s: zero races, zero panics, SequenceNo never 0 after first broadcast

threshold.rationale: Concurrent map/struct access in Broadcast must be fully protected by h.mu

on_exceed: Concurrent access to h.clients map or h.lastPayload without proper locking → 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic → streaming Hub crashes → all dashboard clients lose feed

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Hub.Broadcast called from 50 concurrent goroutines for 20s while GetLastPayload and ClientCount polled simultaneously
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Any race on h.mu or h.lastPayload causes either silent corruption or a runtime panic — both are unacceptable for a live control room
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard operators see inconsistent or corrupt tick payloads — wrong risk scores, wrong topology
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary; also seqNo continuity check catches silent corruption
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: N/A — goroutine lifecycle not tested here; see L3-HUB-003
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: MRSW on h.clients map: Broadcast holds Lock to copy client slice; GetLastPayload holds RLock; concurrent Broadcasts serialise via Lock

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 26 broadcasts with sendBufSize=16, 1 slow (never reads) + 1 fast (drains immediately) client
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Broadcast uses 'select { case c.send <- data: default: h.remove(c) }' — the default branch makes it non-blocking; any block means this code path was not reached
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: One slow browser tab causes ALL dashboard operators to see frozen metrics — control room becomes blind
answered_questions.how_races_were_detected: Go race detector on binary + timing assertions on each Broadcast call
answered_questions.how_leaks_were_detected: ClientCount checked after drops to verify remove() cleaned up correctly
answered_questions.concurrency_pattern_exercised: Non-blocking fan-out: Broadcast iterates snapshot of client list (no lock held) and sends via buffered channel with non-blocking select
